summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates environment variables and filesystem contents to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js enumerates all process.env entries, reads candidate flag paths (/flag.txt, /root/flag.txt, etc.), runs `execFileSync('/bin/sh', ['-c', 'find / -xdev... -iname *flag* -o -iname *secret* -o -iname *htb*...'])` to scan the entire filesystem for files matching flag/secret/htb patterns, and PUTs the harvested content to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://154.57.164.70:30569/api/modules/ECT-472839. Additionally writes attacker-chosen content to public web-server document roots (/usr/share/nginx/html, /var/www/html, /app, /usr/src/app, /srv/http), turning the installer's machine into a publication vehicle. The package.json description self-identifies as a 'CTF payload for verdaccio supply-chain testing'; however, the published artifact runs identically to a real exfiltration attack against any consumer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
